scope.summary: Make the in-product internal UserManual reflect every ModelLane/cloud/local/subagent orchestration behavior this WP introduces, including Flight Recorder/EventLedger linkage and Palmistry/internal_diagnostics posture.
forbidden_paths: manual entries that describe aspirational live APIs as implemented
silent omission of Palmistry/internal_diagnostics because those surfaces are built in another worktree
operator-facing marketing copy instead of operational manual content
treating app/src legacy React/Tauri surfaces as canonical implementation authority
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: There is one in-product internal UserManual surface for this WP; if legacy ModelManual paths/names exist, they are updated as aliases rather than forked into a second manual.

The UserManual covers ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, ModelLaneMessage, ContextBundle handoff, artifact refs, local/cloud/CLI/human/subagent/validator lane launch, cloud ProjectionPlan/ConsentReceipt, advisory-to-authority promotion, recovery/orphan/replay, diagnostics, validation harnesses, and backend navigation introduced by this WP.

Each entry explains purpose, usage path, startup/invocation command or IPC/API path when applicable, expected inputs/outputs, affected tools/features/primitives, durable authority source, failure modes, recovery steps, and verification proof.

Each observable behavior entry links to Flight Recorder/EventLedger evidence and records the HBR-INT-009 posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, and Palmistry as WIRED, NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason, or DEFERRED-with-reason.
Palmistry/internal_diagnostics absence in this worktree is recorded as DEFERRED-with-reason plus the integration follow-up; it is never silently skipped.

UserManual self-consistency proof verifies named commands, IPC channels, backend navigation paths, schema names, storage/event contracts, and diagnostic surfaces exist or are explicitly tagged as roadmap/deferred.

MT-011 proof MUST include a machine-readable per-behavior UserManual coverage matrix keyed by behavior_id/schema/event_family, with columns for implemented command/API/IPC, EventLedger/Flight Recorder evidence path, internal_diagnostics posture, Palmistry posture, and self-consistency result. Keyword grep is supporting evidence only, not acceptance proof.
Every MT that changes product behavior has a corresponding UserManual delta in the same implementation change, and MT-011 verifies coverage by behavior inventory rather than by file-name guesswork.
UserManual entries link each behavior to EventLedger event family/aggregate refs, Flight Recorder trace/span refs, backend navigation paths, diagnostic tier posture, and recovery proof commands.

Palmistry/internal_diagnostics integration is recorded exactly as WIRED, NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason, or DEFERRED-with-reason per behavior; this MT must not claim Palmistry is implemented in this worktree unless product code proves it.
MT-011 owns WP-wide UserManual coverage audit and freshness/self-consistency proof. It does not replace the same-change UserManual obligation in MT-002 through MT-010.

Define and implement Rust-owned coverage matrix schema hsk.user_manual_behavior_coverage@1 generated from ModelLane schema/event/API registries. Required columns: behavior_id, schema_id or event_family, command/API/IPC id, UserManual entry id/version, EventLedger/Flight Recorder evidence path, internal_diagnostics posture, Palmistry posture, self-consistency result, deferred reason, and follow_up_ref.

Coverage proof MUST fail when any behavior lacks a UserManual entry, EventLedger/FR evidence, internal_diagnostics posture, Palmistry posture, runtime route/schema existence, or self-consistency result. Keyword grep is supporting evidence only and cannot be a proof target.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.
scope.risk_if_missed: Future models and the operator will only understand the new orchestration system through this chat or stale packets, causing parallel work, recovery, diagnostics, and cloud/local handoff to drift.

implementation_notes: Start by locating the current in-product manual surface. If the codebase still calls it ModelManual, update that surface and treat ModelManual as a legacy alias; do not create a parallel UserManual directory or feature.
This MT is the WP-wide manual currency pass. It complements MT-010, which adds the lane-specific no-context manual/backend navigation content.

Do not own or rename the internal visual/debug tool in this MT. Use the project-approved name from the separate viewing-tool worktree/session when it exists; otherwise describe the backend navigation equivalent and mark naming as out-of-scope dependency.
Do not build Palmistry or internal_diagnostics here if those surfaces are not in this worktree. Record DEFERRED-with-reason and the follow-up integration hook required by HBR-INT-009/HBR-MAN-004.
Manual entries must cite implemented code truth. If a behavior is deferred, roadmap, or blocked by another worktree, label it that way with reason instead of presenting it as live.

Keep durable state authority clear: PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/ContextBundle/CRDT/Flight Recorder are product authority or evidence surfaces; Markdown/chat/manual prose is a projection and operating guide, not runtime authority.
Treat UserManual as part of product operation, not repo-governance prose. It must be available to future no-context models and operators through product APIs/navigation.
Loom/FEMS/ContextBundle references in manual entries must point to actual backend storage/API surfaces or be explicitly deferred; do not invent visible names from this session.
Manual entries should include enough exact primitives, IDs, commands/routes, expected I/O, failure modes, and recovery steps for a model with no conversation history to use the feature.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

Product implementation for this MT must be Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces where UI is touched. Legacy app/src, app/src-tauri, React, Tauri/WebView, TypeScript, JavaScript, docs/model-manual, and npm proof targets are reference-only or forbidden and MUST NOT satisfy implementation/proof authority.

Proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

risks: Manual can describe the intended orchestration model but miss one concrete primitive, leaving future no-context models unable to recover or use that path.
Palmistry/internal_diagnostics can be omitted because they are being built elsewhere, weakening the project-wide diagnostic invariant.
A second manual surface can be created by accident if legacy ModelManual naming is treated as separate from UserManual.
minimum_controls: Self-consistency proof must scan named primitives, commands, schemas, IPC paths, diagnostic surfaces, and storage/event contracts.
Every observable behavior entry must explicitly record Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry posture.
Legacy ModelManual references must be aliases to the single internal UserManual surface, not a new documentation island.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
product_code_evidence: HBR-MAN-004 requires same-change UserManual updates and diagnostic linkage
src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/user_manual.rs exposes canonical UserManual APIs and legacy alias plan
src/backend/handshake_core/src/flight_recorder/mod.rs includes MemoryPack and Loom event types
src/backend/handshake_core/tests/user_manual_storage_tests.rs states UserManual proof requires live PostgreSQL/EventLedger
patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: Manual currency is a WP-wide acceptance gate: every implemented behavior has a UserManual entry with exact usage, code-truth refs, Flight Recorder/EventLedger evidence, and internal_diagnostics/Palmistry status.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
